name: Regression/ms-operation-versions
description: Content-negotiation conformance for the '$versions' operation, ported from fhir-server's Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E OperationVersionsTests.cs. IMPORTANT: '$versions' is a Microsoft FHIR Server-specific system operation (GET {base}/$versions returning the server's supported FHIR versions) — it is NOT part of the core FHIR specification, so every assertion in this suite is warningOnly. A target server that does not implement this operation will typically return 404, which is itself a perfectly conformant response and should not be treated as a failure. InlineData rows collapsed: 'application/json' + 'application/fhir+json' -> one representative JSON test; 'application/xml' + 'application/fhir+xml' -> one representative XML test; the two invalid Accept values ('application/json1', 'applicaiton/xml') -> one representative invalid-Accept test (both are equally malformed media types and exercise the same 406 code path).

test.name: $versions honors a valid JSON Accept header
test.description: GET {base}/$versions with Accept: application/fhir+json must return HTTP 200 with a Content-Type that echoes the requested media type exactly (source test also covers the bare 'application/json' alias, which is behaviorally equivalent and collapsed here). warningOnly: '$versions' is a proprietary operation that may not exist on the target server.

assert.description: Server should return HTTP 200 (warningOnly: operation may be unimplemented)
Response Content-Type should echo 'application/fhir+json' exactly (warningOnly, see above)

test.name: $versions honors a valid XML Accept header
test.description: GET {base}/$versions with Accept: application/fhir+xml must return HTTP 200 with a Content-Type that echoes the requested media type exactly (source test also covers the bare 'application/xml' alias, collapsed here). warningOnly: '$versions' is a proprietary operation that may not exist on the target server, and some servers only support the JSON format.

assert.description: Server should return HTTP 200 (warningOnly: operation and/or XML format may be unimplemented)
Response Content-Type should echo 'application/fhir+xml' exactly (warningOnly, see above)

test.name: $versions with no Accept header defaults to application/fhir+json
test.description: GET {base}/$versions with no Accept header at all must return HTTP 200 with Content-Type application/fhir+json (the server's default representation). warningOnly: '$versions' is a proprietary operation that may not exist on the target server.

assert.description: Server should return HTTP 200 (warningOnly: operation may be unimplemented)
Response Content-Type should default to application/fhir+json (warningOnly, see above)

test.name: $versions rejects an invalid Accept header with 406
test.description: GET {base}/$versions with a malformed Accept header (e.g. 'application/json1') must return HTTP 406 Not Acceptable (source test also covers the typo'd 'applicaiton/xml' value, collapsed here since both exercise the same invalid-media-type rejection path). warningOnly: '$versions' is a proprietary operation that may not exist on the target server (in which case 404 is expected instead of 406).

operation.description: GET /$versions with an invalid Accept header value

assert.description: Server should return HTTP 406 Not Acceptable (warningOnly: operation may be unimplemented, or may 404 instead)
